error_resilience.overview: Bob analysis is most useful when *something is wrong* — failing tests, broken builds, problematic diffs. So the stages that call Bob have to be resilient to earlier-stage failures and to Bob's own occasional flakes. This guide covers the standard patterns: catchError around Bob calls, post blocks for cleanup and archiving, and resilient input gathering.

core_principle.title: Bob analysis should not block the pipeline
core_principle.description: A failure in Bob (network blip, mode misconfiguration, prompt too large) should mark the build UNSTABLE, not abort it. The earlier stages — Maven build, tests — are what determine pipeline correctness. Bob is commentary on top.

pattern.name: catcherror_unstable
pattern.description: Wrap askBob in catchError. If Bob fails, the stage is marked FAILURE but the overall build becomes UNSTABLE and the pipeline continues to the next stage.

pattern.name: post_always_archives
pattern.description: Move archiveArtifacts into post { always { ... } } so artifacts get archived even when the main steps throw (e.g., Bob threw, the writeFile happened, but the stage failed before archiveArtifacts ran).

pattern.name: resilient_input_gathering
pattern.description: Earlier stages may have failed. Make Bob's inputs (diffs, logs, scan reports) fall back to empty rather than throwing. Bob can analyze "nothing changed" gracefully; it can't analyze a NoSuchFileException.

pattern.name: run_after_failure
pattern.description: For stages whose whole purpose is to analyze a failure (test failure analysis, build failure diagnosis), use catchError on the *upstream* stage — let it record the failure and mark the build UNSTABLE — and then let the Bob analysis stage run normally on the next iteration.

pattern.name: timeout
pattern.description: Cap Bob calls so a stuck request doesn't hold the entire pipeline. 5 minutes is enough for nearly any analysis; if you find Bob taking longer, the prompt is probably too big.

practice.title: Always wrap askBob in catchError (with buildResult: 'UNSTABLE')
practice.rationale: Without it, a Bob failure aborts the build. With it, the build goes UNSTABLE and downstream stages still run.

practice.title: Archive in post { always { ... } }, not in steps
practice.rationale: archiveArtifacts in post always runs, even on stage failure. If you put it in steps and the stage throws first, you lose the artifact.

practice.title: Make input collection resilient with `|| true` or `|| : > file`
practice.rationale: A diff that fails or a missing test report shouldn't kill the analysis stage — Bob can describe an empty input.

practice.title: Set a timeout
practice.rationale: 5 minutes is plenty. A stuck askBob call without a timeout will eventually hit the pipeline-level timeout, but that's a worse failure mode.

issue.name: pipeline_aborted_on_bob_failure
issue.symptom: Pipeline goes red and stops at the Bob stage.
issue.cause: askBob threw and there's no catchError around it.

issue.name: artifact_missing_on_failure
issue.symptom: Build is UNSTABLE; the Bob output file is in the workspace but not in Build Artifacts.
issue.cause: archiveArtifacts is in steps and never ran because the stage failed first.

issue.name: diff_stage_fails_on_main_branch_build
issue.symptom: git diff origin/main...HEAD fails or returns empty when building main itself.
issue.fix: Use `|| : > git-diff.txt` to fall back to an empty file. Bob will respond with "nothing notable changed" — which is correct.

issue.name: prompt_too_large
issue.symptom: askBob times out or returns a generic apology.
issue.cause: The diff or log you handed Bob is huge.
issue.fix: Trim the input — last N lines of a log, only changed files in a diff, summarize before sending. See 2_file_based_prompts.xml for techniques.

quick_reference.command: post { always { archiveArtifacts artifacts: 'bob-*.md', allowEmptyArchive: true } }
